signature_wines.name: Tío Pepe Fino
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Bone dry and pale with green apple, fresh almond, sea breeze and chamomile aromatics; the solera ageing under a thick flor yeast blanket gives a consistently saline, refreshing finish.
signature_wines.region_context: Uno de los Finos más distribuidos del mundo; la expresión bajo-albariza y flor que define el estilo canónico de Jerez.

verified.address_quoted: Manuel María González 12, 11403 Jerez de la Frontera, Cádiz

signature_wines.name: Inocente Fino
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Single-vineyard Fino from the Macharnudo Alto albariza pago, fermented in seasoned American oak butts without blending from other plots. Fresh almonds, apple blossom, beeswax and a long, focused clean finish.
signature_wines.region_context: The only single-vineyard, single-pago Fino aged exclusively in oak; a study in Macharnudo terroir that stands apart from multi-pago blends.

verified.address_quoted: Calle Pizarro 10, 11402 Jerez de la Frontera, Cádiz

signature_wines.name: La Gitana Manzanilla
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Delicate and saline with chamomile, sea spray, fresh apple and a briny Atlantic finish; the Miraflores pago in Sanlúcar provides a fine-boned flor character that is lighter and more fragrant than Jerez Finos.
signature_wines.region_context: Sanlúcar's maritime microclimate produces thinner, more active flor; La Gitana is the entry point for Manzanilla worldwide, showing the appellation style at accessible price.

verified.address_quoted: Banda Playa 42, 11540 Sanlúcar de Barrameda, Cádiz

signature_wines.name: Goya XL Manzanilla En Rama
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Unfiltered and unlightened en rama release from the La Goya solera; richer in texture and flor-yeast character than the standard La Goya, with live yeast, sea air, fuller doughy body and vibrant citrus oil.
signature_wines.region_context: The en rama format captures Manzanilla closest to its cellar state; the spring release timing coincides with maximum flor activity after the cool Sanlúcar winter.

verified.address_quoted: Juan Antonio Pacheco 12, 11540 Sanlúcar de Barrameda, Cádiz

signature_wines.name: NPU Amontillado VORS
signature_wines.vintage_range: NV VORS (minimum 30 years average solera age; solera since 1922)
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Deep amber with old walnut, dried orange rind, antique furniture wax and tobacco on the nose; the palate brings bitter almond oil, toffee and woody spice before a very long, austere dry finish.
signature_wines.region_context: VORS (Very Old Rare Sherry) classification requires a minimum 30-year average age certified by the Consejo Regulador. NPU is one of Jerez's longest-established Amontillado soleras.

verified.address_quoted: Lealas 26-30, 11403 Jerez de la Frontera, Cádiz

signature_wines.name: Capataz Andrés Amontillado
signature_wines.vintage_range: NV Solera Reserva (average 12 years dual ageing; first bottling 1978)
signature_wines.price_note: Part of the Lustau Solera Reserva range, an accessible introduction to aged Amontillado style.
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Complex Solera Reserva Amontillado with dual ageing: first biological under flor, then oxidative in American oak. Toasted hazelnuts, toffee, tangerine peel and dry spice; walnut and bitter caramel on the finish.
signature_wines.region_context: Lustau's Solera Reserva tier showcases aged Amontillado character at mid-range pricing, demonstrating the hazelnut-and-toffee profile that emerges from prolonged dual-ageing.

verified.address_quoted: Plaza del Cubo 4, 11403 Jerez de la Frontera, Cádiz

signature_wines.name: Faraón Oloroso
signature_wines.vintage_range: NV VORS (30-plus years average solera age; house founded 1792)
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Intensely concentrated VORS Oloroso from Sanlúcar with dark dried fruit, roasted coffee, toasted oak and leather; the characteristic saline mineral of the appellation persists through the full oxidative weight of 30-plus years.
signature_wines.region_context: Faraón demonstrates that Oloroso aged in Sanlúcar carries a distinct saline depth from the Atlantic influence, contrasting with the drier, more austere profile of inland Jerez Olorosos.

verified.address_quoted: Banda Playa 42, 11540 Sanlúcar de Barrameda, Cádiz

signature_wines.name: Don Nuño Oloroso
signature_wines.vintage_range: NV Solera Reserva (average 10 years oxidative ageing; first bottling 1980)
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Rich, full Oloroso from the Lustau Solera Reserva: dark amber with roasted walnuts, tobacco, dark dried fruit and toasted oak on the nose; dark coffee, caramel, dried plum and smoky wood spice on a long dry finish.
signature_wines.region_context: Don Nuño offers an accessible entry into dry Oloroso, showing the characteristic nutty-oxidative depth of Jerez without the age premium of VORS bottlings.

verified.address_quoted: Plaza del Cubo 4, 11403 Jerez de la Frontera, Cádiz

signature_wines.name: Peninsula Palo Cortado
signature_wines.vintage_range: NV Solera Reserva (average 15 years; first bottling 1985)
signature_wines.tasting_notes: A well-documented example of Palo Cortado: Amontillado-like elegance on the nose with orange bitters, mahogany and roasted hazelnuts; Oloroso depth on the palate with bitter walnut, dry orange peel, dark spice and a long tea-tannin finish.
signature_wines.region_context: Palo Cortado is the rarest Sherry style, arising when a wine developing under flor unexpectedly shifts to oxidative ageing. Peninsula is a reliable expression of this dual character.

verified.address_quoted: Plaza del Cubo 4, 11403 Jerez de la Frontera, Cádiz

signature_wines.name: Noé Pedro Ximénez VORS
signature_wines.vintage_range: NV VORS (minimum 30 years average solera age; solera since 1830)
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Near-black and viscous VORS PX of extreme concentration: compressed dried raisins, tar, black coffee, leather and dried tobacco on the nose; the palate delivers tar, black figs, treacle and salted caramel with an extraordinarily long, glycerol-rich finish.
signature_wines.region_context: The VORS minimum 30-year age requirement, combined with the natural grape-raisin sweetness of sun-dried Pedro Ximénez, produces a depth of concentration that cannot be replicated in younger PX bottlings.

verified.address_quoted: Manuel María González 12, 11403 Jerez de la Frontera, Cádiz

signature_wines.name: Cardenal Cisneros Pedro Ximénez
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Rich, intensely sweet PX from a century-old solera: deep black-brown with dark raisins, roasted coffee, toffee and dates on the nose; concentrated figs, dark molasses, caramel and warm spice on a long warm finish.
signature_wines.region_context: Named in honour of Cardinal Cisneros, this PX is an accessible and deeply traditional expression of the sun-dried Pedro Ximénez grape that has been central to the Jerez sweet-wine tradition for generations.

verified.address_quoted: Lealas 26-30, 11403 Jerez de la Frontera, Cádiz

signature_wines.name: La Bota de Fino
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Single-cask unfiltered Fino selected from an exceptional bota in a Jerez bodega: chamomile, toasted almonds, beeswax and light herbs on the nose; saline minerals, lemon zest, almond cream and natural flor complexity on the palate.
signature_wines.region_context: Equipo Navazos was founded by a group of Sherry scholars who began sourcing and bottling exceptional individual casks direct from bodegas in 2005. Their numbered La Bota series brought single-cask Sherry to wider specialist attention.

verified.address_quoted: C/ Divina Pastora 2, 11403 Jerez de la Frontera, Cádiz
